perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall not be entitled to copy any Confidential Information furnished by the Disclosing Party hereunder, unless and to the extent it is necessary for the purpose of this Agreement. Upon request of the Disclosing Party or upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall return, delete or destroy (as instructed in writing by the Disclosing Party) all Confidential Information, and all copies thereof. The Receiving Party shall certify in writing that the information has been returned, deleted or destroyed (as applicable) and that no copies have been retained of any such Confidential Information, either partly or wholly.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, except for derivative works created by the Receiving Party incorporating the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall be entitled to copy any Confidential Information furnished by the Disclosing Party hereunder, if necessary for internal documentation purposes. Upon termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall make reasonable efforts to return, delete or destroy (as instructed in writing by the Disclosing Party) all Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, to the extent reasonably practical. The Receiving Party will provide a statement that it has made efforts to return, delete, or destroy as much information as it could, either partly or wholly.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and that the Receiving Party shall not be entitled to copy it unless necessary for the purpose of the agreement and that shall return it and 'certify in writing'. However, the modified text introduces contradictions: (1) It allows for derivative works to be an exception to the Disclosing Party's ownership; (2) It enables the Receiving Party to copy for internal documentation regardless of the 'purpose of this Agreement'; and (3) It changes the return obligation from a strict requirement to 'reasonable efforts'. These create internal contradictions because previously, the receiving party had no rights to copy or ownership and had to do an strict return of the confidential information, now it has some right over it. These contradicts the strict restriction for its usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, except for derivative works created by the Receiving Party incorporating the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall be entitled to copy any Confidential Information furnished by the Disclosing Party hereunder, if necessary for internal documentation purposes. Upon termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall make reasonable efforts to return, delete or destroy (as instructed in writing by the Disclosing Party) all Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, to the extent reasonably practical. The Receiving Party will provide a statement that it has made efforts to return, delete, or destroy as much information as it could, either partly or wholly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces exceptions to the Disclosing Party's ownership of Confidential Information and allows the Receiving Party to copy it for internal documentation, contradicting the original strict restrictions. Additionally, it weakens the obligation to return confidential information to a 'reasonable efforts' standard, further contradicting the original requirement for a strict return and certification. The Contradicted Text is equal to the Changed Text, because the same section has the contradicting concepts.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information (including but not limited to all intellectual property rights emanating from or related to Confidential Information) remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement grants any right or license in the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information (excluding any improvements or modifications developed by the Receiving Party using the Confidential Information) remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party retains a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such improvements or modifications for its internal business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that all Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, and the Receiving Party is not granted any rights or licenses. The modified version states the Receiving Party can retain 'a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such improvements or modifications for its internal business purposes.' This clause provides the Receiving Party specific rights to use the derived intellectual property. This change directly conflicts with the original statement that ‘nothing in this Agreement grants any right or license in the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party.’
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information (excluding any improvements or modifications developed by the Receiving Party using the Confidential Information) remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party retains a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such improvements or modifications for its internal business purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, with no rights or licenses granted to the Receiving Party. The changed text contradicts this by granting the Receiving Party a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use improvements or modifications derived from the Confidential Information for its internal business purposes. This creates a direct conflict with the original statement, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In the case of any breach of any material obligations under this Agreement by the Receiving Party or any permitted third party who has received Confidential Information according to this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall be liable to pay liquidated damages to the Disclosing Party amounting to SEK [amount] for each such breach of its obligations. Notwithstanding the right to receive liquidated damages, the Disclosing Party has the right to take any other legal measures available and to claim and receive compensation for damages exceeding any liquidated damages, including but not limited to loss of profit.
perturbation.changed_text: In the case of any breach of confidentiality under this Agreement by the Receiving Party or any permitted third party, the Parties agree to first attempt resolution through mediation before pursuing legal action. Liquidated damages shall not apply. Instead, the Disclosing Party's sole remedy shall be to seek injunctive relief to prevent further unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original agreement specifies liquidated damages of 'SEK [amount]' for breaches of material obligations, and the Disclosing Party retains the right to pursue legal measures and damages exceeding the liquidated amount. The modified version eliminates liquidated damages, requires mandatory mediation before legal action, and limits the Disclosing Party's remedy to injunctive relief. This contradicts the Disclosing Party's original right to pursue monetary damages and additional legal measures, creating uncertainty about the consequences of breaching the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the case of any breach of confidentiality under this Agreement by the Receiving Party or any permitted third party, the Parties agree to first attempt resolution through mediation before pursuing legal action. Liquidated damages shall not apply. Instead, the Disclosing Party's sole remedy shall be to seek injunctive relief to prevent further unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text completely alters the remedies available to the Disclosing Party in case of a breach of confidentiality. The original text allowed for liquidated damages and additional legal measures, while the changed text eliminates liquidated damages and limits the remedy to injunctive relief only. This creates a direct contradiction with the previous right to pursue monetary damages, which constitutes a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
